evidence: Reviewed the displayed equation together with its definitions and derivation in the source PDF.
Symbols, indices, domains, and dependencies were checked against neighboring equations and prose.

formula:0001.note: Confirmed as the best-ask definition, with n+1 as the empty-ask sentinel.

formula:0002.note: Confirmed as the best-bid definition, with zero as the empty-bid sentinel.

formula:0003.note: Confirmed from the preceding best-quote definitions: the midpoint is their average and the spread their difference.

evidence: The paper defines X_p(t)<0 for bid orders and says -X_p(t) is the number of bid orders.
Q_i^B is explicitly introduced as the number of buy orders and is used as a nonnegative queue size later.
The printed expression omits the minus sign and would make every nonempty bid queue negative.
formula:0004.note: Corrected a high-confidence sign typo: bid depth must be -X at the corresponding price because bid states are negative by definition.

formula:0005.note: Confirmed as the nonnegative ask-depth definition at distance i from the bid.

formula:0006.note: Removed OCR spillover and reconstructed the state update with the unit entry in component p.

formula:0007.note: Confirmed as the power-law specification for limit-order arrival intensity versus quote distance.

formula:0008.latex: \begin{aligned}x&\to x_{p-1}&&\text{at rate }\lambda(p_A(t)-p),&&p<p_A(t),\\x&\to x_{p+1}&&\text{at rate }\lambda(p-p_B(t)),&&p>p_B(t),\\x&\to x_{p_B(t)+1}&&\text{at rate }\mu,\\x&\to x_{p_A(t)-1}&&\text{at rate }\mu,\\x&\to x_{p+1}&&\text{at rate }\theta(p_A(t)-p)|x_p|,&&p<p_A(t),\\x&\to x_{p-1}&&\text{at rate }\theta(p-p_B(t))|x_p|,&&p>p_B(t).\end{aligned}
formula:0008.note: Confirmed all six Markov-chain transitions against the sign convention for bid and ask inventory and the stated event intensities.

formula:0009.note: Confirmed as the convergent birth-death recurrence-series bound using mu_i at least i theta.

formula:0010.note: Removed OCR prose contamination and restored the divergent lower bound used in the ergodicity criterion.

formula:0011.note: Confirmed as the observed limit-order count per sample duration.

formula:0012.note: Confirmed as the fitted extrapolation of the empirical arrival-rate curve.

formula:0013.note: Confirmed as the least-squares objective used to estimate the power-law parameters.

formula:0014.note: Confirmed dimensionally as market-order arrivals per time rescaled from average market-order size to the model's limit-order unit.

formula:0015.note: Confirmed as average empirical bid depth measured in average-limit-order units.

formula:0016.note: Confirmed as cancellations per unit time and standing depth, with size rescaling and a flat tail beyond five ticks.

formula:0017.note: Confirmed as the paper's two-sided Laplace-transform convention.

formula:0018.note: Confirmed as the expectation form of the transform for a random variable X.

formula:0019.latex: \widehat f_{X+Y}(s)=\mathbb E[e^{-s(X+Y)}]=\mathbb E[e^{-sX}]\mathbb E[e^{-sY}]=\widehat f_X(s)\widehat f_Y(s)
formula:0019.note: Confirmed from independence as the product rule for the transform of X+Y.

formula:0020.note: Confirmed as the Bromwich inversion integral under the stated integrability condition.

formula:0021.latex: w_n=t_1\circ t_2\circ\cdots\circ t_n(0),\quad n\ge1,\qquad t_k(u)=\frac{a_k}{b_k+u},\quad k\ge1
formula:0021.note: Confirmed as the composition definition of the continued-fraction convergents.

formula:0022.note: Confirmed as the paper's notation for the limiting continued fraction.

formula:0023.note: Confirmed by the strong Markov decomposition of the passage from b to zero into successive one-level passage times.

formula:0024.note: Removed duplicated OCR fragments and retained the transform product implied by independence of the passage-time increments.

formula:0025.note: Confirmed as the continued-fraction representation for a one-level birth-death first-passage transform.

formula:0026.note: Removed OCR prose spillover and combined the preceding product and continued-fraction formulas.

formula:0027.note: Confirmed as the first time at which the mid-price differs from its initial value.

formula:0028.note: Confirmed as the conditional probability of an upward first mid-price move.

evidence: The function is indexed by the initial queue size j and the prefactor has exponent j.
Equation (7), from which this specialization is taken, has the same initial-state value as the upper product limit.
The symbol b is not bound in the proposition's definition of f_{j,S}.
formula:0029.note: Corrected the printed upper product limit b to j so the transform depends consistently on its declared initial queue size.

evidence: An upward move is the event that the ask-side stopping time, initialized at a, precedes the bid-side stopping time initialized at b.
The transform of U-V must use the a transform at s and the b transform at -s.
The paper's immediately following S=1 reduction uses f_{a,1}(s)f_{b,1}(-s), contradicting the printed order in equation (10).
formula:0030.note: Corrected a high-confidence a/b swap in equation (10); the ordering now agrees with the event definition, transform sign, and equation (11).

formula:0031.note: Restored the a-then-b ordering visible in equation (11), consistent with the transform of the ask-minus-bid passage-time difference.

formula:0032.note: Removed OCR debris and reconstructed the minimum over queue-depletion and inside-spread arrival times.

formula:0033.note: Removed a duplicated OCR line and retained the equivalent minimum-event representation for an upward move.

formula:0034.note: Confirmed as the Laplace transform of the minimum between the passage time sigma_B and an independent exponential clock.

formula:0035.note: Confirmed from independence and the exponential survival function.

formula:0036.note: Confirmed by differentiating the preceding cdf of the minimum.

evidence: Lemma 4 defines the random variable as sigma_B wedge Z, so its density must carry the same subscript.
Every integral is over t and the integrands are functions of t; the printed ds in the second line is inconsistent.
The corrected integral evaluates by integration by parts to the transform stated in the lemma.
formula:0037.note: Corrected two high-confidence notation typos in the derivation: the density is for sigma_B wedge Z and the integration variable is dt.

formula:0038.note: Confirmed as the probability that a noncanceling bid order executes before the first mid-price move.

formula:0039.note: Confirmed as the transform of a sum of j independent exponential service times with the stated state-dependent rates.

evidence: The execution time epsilon_B starts from bid size b and is represented by g_{b,S}.
Its competing ask-depletion time sigma_A starts from ask size a and therefore requires f_{a,S}.
The paper's S=1 reduction in equation (16) uses g_{b,1}(s)f_{a,1}(-s), confirming the a subscript.
formula:0040.note: Corrected the competing passage-time transform from f_b to f_a so equation (15) agrees with its event definition and equation (16).

formula:0041.note: Confirmed as the unit-spread reduction combining bid execution time with the competing ask-queue passage time.

formula:0042.note: Confirmed as the conditional probability that both noncanceling orders execute before the mid-price changes.

formula:0043.note: Confirmed as the decomposition over residual queue states and the bid-order execution time.

formula:0044.note: Separated equation (19) from OCR spillover belonging to equation (20) and retained the Poisson transition probability and its mean.

formula:0045.note: Confirmed as the pure-death chain transition probability obtained from the matrix exponential.

formula:0046.note: Restored the separate subdiagonal and diagonal entries of the pure-death generator; OCR had merged adjacent matrix columns.

formula:0047.note: Removed OCR debris and retained the equivalent unit-spread event that both orders execute before either quote depletes.

formula:0048.note: Confirmed by partitioning the joint execution event according to which order executes first and using the queue-dominance relations from Lemma 2.

formula:0049.note: Removed a duplicated OCR fragment and retained the conditioning integral over the bid-order execution time.

formula:0050.latex: \begin{aligned}\mathbb P[\epsilon_B<\epsilon_A<\sigma_B\mid\epsilon_B=t]&=\sum_{i=0}^{\infty}\sum_{j=0}^{a}\mathbb P[\epsilon_B<\epsilon_A<\sigma_B\mid\epsilon_B=t,\widetilde X_B(t)=i,\widetilde W_A(t)=j]\,\mathbb P[\widetilde X_B(t)=i,\widetilde W_A(t)=j\mid\epsilon_B=t]\\&=\sum_{i=0}^{\infty}\sum_{j=1}^{a}\mathbb P[\epsilon_j<\sigma_i]\,\mathbb P[\widetilde X_B(t)=i\mid\epsilon_B=t]\,\mathbb P[\widetilde W_A(t)=j\mid\epsilon_B=t]\\&=\sum_{i=0}^{\infty}\sum_{j=1}^{a}\mathbb P[\epsilon_j<\sigma_i]\,\mathbb P[\widetilde X_B(t)=i\mid\epsilon_B=t]\,\mathbb P[\widetilde W_A(t)=j].\end{aligned}
formula:0050.note: Reconstructed equation (25) from the conditioning argument; the final equality uses independence of the ask pure-death process from epsilon_B.

formula:0051.note: Removed severe OCR repetition and restored the Tonelli-interchanged sum and integral derived from equations (23)-(25).

formula:0052.note: Removed section-heading spillover and retained the ergodic time-average limit used for stationary expectations.

formula:0053.note: Removed OCR prose spillover and restored the realized-volatility definition from successive mid-price log returns.

formula:0054.note: Confirmed as the recursive event-time definition; the inequality is required for the next state change.

formula:0055.note: Reconstructed equation (27): each branch is the arrival intensity divided by the total intensity of the next change at that queue.

formula:0056.note: Confirmed as the pooled empirical frequency of upward queue-size changes conditional on a change.

formula:0057.latex: \begin{aligned}\widehat B_{up}&=\left|\{m:\widehat Q_i^B(\widehat T_m)=n,\ \widehat Q_i^B(\widehat T_{m+1})>n\}\right|,\\\widehat A_{up}&=\left|\{m:\widehat Q_i^A(\widehat T_m)=n,\ \widehat Q_i^A(\widehat T_{m+1})>n\}\right|,\\\widehat B_{change}&=\left|\{m:\widehat Q_i^B(\widehat T_m)=n,\ \widehat Q_i^B(\widehat T_{m+1})\ne n\}\right|,\\\widehat A_{change}&=\left|\{m:\widehat Q_i^A(\widehat T_m)=n,\ \widehat Q_i^A(\widehat T_{m+1})\ne n\}\right|.\end{aligned}
formula:0057.note: Removed OCR prose and restored the four empirical event-count definitions; change counts use non-equality, not equality.

formula:0002.note: Confirmed as the semi-affine special case of the preceding input-output map.

formula:0004.note: Corrected the OCR loss of the minus sign in t-1; the recurrence and surrounding prose require the previous hidden state.

formula:0005.note: Confirmed as the continuous-output affine readout from the final hidden state.

evidence: Equation (2) defines F_y(h_T)=W_yh_T+b_y as the affine logits/readout.
For a categorical output, probabilities are obtained by applying softmax to those logits.
The printed chained equality identifies the affine logits with their softmax and is not generally true.
formula:0006.note: Corrected a high-confidence paper notation error by applying softmax to the affine readout without equating logits and probabilities.

formula:0007.note: Normalized the optimization display and confirmed the empirical loss plus regularization objective.

formula:0009.note: Confirmed as the midpoint of the level-one best ask and bid prices.

formula:0010.note: Normalized the architecture display and confirmed the K-class softmax readout and ReLU recurrent state equation.

formula:0011.note: Confirmed the standard harmonic-mean F1 formula. The paper's nearby prose calls it a geometric mean, but the displayed equation itself is correct.
